parsed_review.summary: CP00-841 is a descriptor-only RP27 Platform Extensibility "Claude review permission/audit bridge" pack (10 units, risk A, range RP27.P09.M05.S06-S15; deliverables ui:1/implementation:8/claude_review:1), inspected via Read/Grep/Glob only. Coverage matches the live plan: included units, titles (CLAUDE_REVIEW_ROWS.slice(5,15) = "UI leak questions"..."PASS closeout note"), and deliverable/phase/micro/title distributions all align with PLATFORM_EXTENSIBILITY_CP841_REQUIREMENTS, and validators (validators.js:2292/2328/2383) enforce these plus per-row no-leak guards (descriptor_only=true, runtime_execution=false; no API-key/webhook/workflow/permission-detail/audit-body/real-client/credential payloads; rejects api_key_issued/webhook_delivered/workflow_executed/permission_decision_written/audit_event_written/writes_product_state=true). createRow defaults (registry.js:1879) and PLATFORM_EXTENSIBILITY_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION back these. The generated contract is consistent (current/latest_pack CP00-841, latest_projection descriptor PlatformExtensibilityCp841ClaudeReviewPermissionAuditBridgeDescriptor, validation.valid=true with no_write_attestation all false); a grep found no dangerous *_included/*_written/*_issued/runtime_execution/enterprise-trust flags set true anywhere in the contract. Tests (model.test.js:660-692) assert zero validation errors, runtime-closed flags, read_only Claude packet, non-final-approval, and contract projection currency; command-evidence reports node --check, npm run rp27:platform-extensibility:validate, and platform node --test at exit 0 (not re-run here, per read-only). Authority boundary preserved: Claude read-only and not final approver (claude_packet.read_only=true, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, authority_boundary.claude_is_final_approval=false), human approval required for runtime opening, enterprise trust not claimed. Hermes packet is deterministic review evidence only (gate H27, emits_runtime_receipt=false), not product runtime. Manifest holds production_ready=false/runtime_ready=false/status=in_progress pending exactly this review; the descriptor-verified scope (not runtime/enterprise trust) is supported. One P3 nit: an inert closeoutOverride branch in the CP841 case-set builder. No P0/P1/P2. Does not block pack closeout or this CP's goal closeout; final human approval for runtime opening remains outside this receipt.

findings.title: Inert closeoutOverride branch in CP841 case-set builder
findings.details: createPlatformExtensibilityCp841ClaudeReviewPermissionAuditBridgeCaseSet computes closeoutOverride (to_pack_id/next_subphase_id) and applies it only when a row title === 'Closeout handoff' (registry.js:2453). CP841's required_section_rows are CLAUDE_REVIEW_ROWS.slice(5,15) ('UI leak questions'...'PASS closeout note'), and 'Closeout handoff' is not even a member of CLAUDE_REVIEW_ROWS, so the override is always inert for this pack. Closeout routing is instead provided and independently validated via createPlatformExtensibilityCp841CloseoutHandoff() (registry.js:3316) and the descriptor.closeout_handoff check (validators.js:2376). Harmless shared-template reuse with no correctness, no-leak, no-write, or routing impact.
findings.recommendation: Optional only: drop the unused closeoutOverride in this builder, or keep it for cross-pack template uniformity. No action required for closeout.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking. Accepted as harmless template code; does not affect descriptor correctness, boundary guarantees, or closeout routing. No fix required before closeout.
